questions.answer: Recursion is a programming technique where a function calls itself in its definition.

questions.q: Write a recursive function to calculate the factorial of a number.
What does the time complexity of a recursive function represent?
questions.answer: The time complexity of a recursive function represents the amount of time the function takes to execute as the input size increases.

questions.q: Write a recursive function to find the largest element in an array.
A function has a base case, but it does not have a recursive call. What will happen?
questions.answer: The function will not execute and will result in a stack overflow error.

questions.q: What is tail recursion in programming?
questions.answer: Tail recursion is a type of recursion where the last operation performed by the function is the recursive call.

questions.q: Write a recursive function to convert a binary number to decimal.
What is the difference between direct and indirect recursion?
questions.answer: Direct recursion is when a function calls itself directly, while indirect recursion is when a function calls another function that eventually calls the original function.

questions.q: A recursive function has a time complexity of O(2^n). What does this mean?
questions.answer: This means the function takes twice as long to execute for each increase in input size by 1.

questions.q: Write a recursive function to find the sum of all elements in an array.
